entries.description: - Revolves around Bertrand Russel’s quest to find the foundation of Mathematics, as investigated by modern artists - Bertrands infatuation with Logic/Mathematics was a result realising its ability to provide certainty in a constrained domain, when he was trying to make sense of the uncertainty around him during his childhood. His pursuit of contributing to this field and belief in its ability to  be applied to to other parts of life to provide absolute certainty was eventually met with disappointment, as reflected in his personal life and attempts to dissuade Britain from engaging in WW1. Gödel’s incompleteness theorems solidified this. - The comic explores incidence of insanity among mathematicians and questions if one if a precondition or the result of the other. It provides a theory where mathematicians create and work on an abstract world free of constrains of human life, but have the potential to confuse this with real life. - The inclusion of Oresteia in the narrative offers an alternative to resolving the messy irrational tribulations of human life, with community, discourse and compromise, implying the inability of mathematics in solving such problems.
entries.title: Logicomix
